questions.text: What is the total area of India's landmass?
questions.explanation: India's total area is approximately 3.28 million square kilometers, which is about 2.4% of the world's total geographical area.

questions.text: Which of the following latitudes passes through the center of India?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer (23°30' N) passes almost halfway through the country.

questions.text: What is the latitudinal extent of the mainland of India?
questions.explanation: The mainland of India extends between latitudes 8°4'N and 37°6'N.

questions.text: The Standard Meridian of India passes through which of these cities?
questions.explanation: The Standard Meridian of India (82°30'E) passing through Mirzapur in Uttar Pradesh is taken as the standard time for the whole country.

questions.text: What is India's rank in the world in terms of total area?
questions.explanation: India is the seventh-largest country in the world by area.

questions.text: Which island group of India lies to its southeast in the Bay of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Andaman and Nicobar islands lie in the Bay of Bengal to the southeast of the Indian mainland.

questions.text: The longitudinal extent of India is:
questions.explanation: India's longitudinal extent is from 68°7'E to 97°25'E.

questions.text: What is the time lag between Gujarat and Arunachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Due to the longitudinal extent of nearly 30 degrees, there is a time lag of two hours between the westernmost and easternmost points.

questions.text: The southernmost point of the Indian Union, Indira Point, got submerged under water in:
questions.explanation: Indira Point was submerged in the sea water in 2004 during the Tsunami.

questions.text: India's land boundary is approximately:
questions.explanation: India has a land boundary of about 15,200 km.

questions.text: Which of the following separates Sri Lanka from India?
options: Palk Strait and Gulf of Mannar
questions.explanation: Sri Lanka is separated from India by a narrow channel of sea formed by the Palk Strait and the Gulf of Mannar.

questions.text: Before 1947, there were two types of states in India: provinces and ________.
questions.explanation: Provinces were ruled directly by British officials, and Princely states were ruled by local, hereditary rulers.

questions.text: India's contact with the world through land routes is ________ than her maritime contacts.
questions.explanation: The various passes across the mountains in the north provided passages to ancient travellers long before ocean routes were known.

questions.text: In which year did the Suez Canal open, reducing India's distance from Europe by 7,000 km?
questions.explanation: The opening of the Suez Canal in 1869 reduced the travel distance between India and Europe.

questions.text: Which neighbor of India is an island country located to the south of Lakshadweep?
questions.explanation: The Maldives Islands are situated to the south of the Lakshadweep Islands.

questions.text: Which of the following states does NOT share a boundary with Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Punjab share borders with Pakistan, but Haryana is an inland state.

questions.text: What is the total length of India's coastline, including the island groups?
questions.explanation: The total length of the coastline of the mainland, including Andaman and Nicobar and Lakshadweep, is 7,516.6 km.

questions.text: Which of these countries shares the longest land boundary with India?
questions.explanation: Although all four are neighbors, Bangladesh shares the longest land border with India.

questions.text: India is located in which hemisphere?
questions.explanation: India lies entirely in the Northern hemisphere, and longitudinally it is in the Eastern hemisphere.

questions.text: The North-South distance of India is approximately:
questions.explanation: The actual distance from the north (Laddakh) to the south (Kanyakumari) is 3,214 km.

questions.text: Which state of India shares a border with China, Nepal, and Bhutan?
questions.explanation: Sikkim is bordered by Nepal to the west, Bhutan to the east, and China to the north.

questions.text: The East-West distance of India is approximately:
questions.explanation: The distance from Gujarat in the west to Arunachal Pradesh in the east is 2,933 km.

questions.text: Which of the following is the smallest state in India by area?
questions.explanation: Goa is the smallest state in India.

questions.text: India’s neighbors in the north-west are:
questions.explanation: India shares its land boundaries with Pakistan and Afghanistan in the north-west.

questions.text: Kanyakumari is situated on the ________ coast.
questions.explanation: Kanyakumari is the southernmost tip of the Indian mainland.

questions.text: The sun rises two hours earlier in Arunachal Pradesh as compared to Gujarat. What is used to keep the time uniform?
questions.explanation: IST, based on the 82°30'E meridian, is used to avoid confusion caused by different local times across longitudes.

questions.text: Which of the following describes India's position in Asia?
options: Southward extension of the Asian continent
questions.explanation: The Indian landmass has a central location between the East and the West Asia. India is a southward extension of the Asian continent.

questions.text: Which water body lies to the west of the Indian peninsula?
questions.explanation: The Arabian Sea lies to the west of India.

questions.text: My friend hails from a country which does not share a land boundary with India. Identify the country.
questions.explanation: Tajikistan does not share a land boundary with India, whereas Bhutan, Bangladesh, and Nepal do.

questions.text: The Tropic of Cancer does not pass through:
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram. It does not pass through Odisha.

questions.text: The easternmost longitude of India is:
questions.explanation: The easternmost longitude of India is 97°25'E in Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.text: Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, West Bengal and Sikkim have common frontiers with:
questions.explanation: All these five states share a border with Nepal.

questions.text: Which state of India is surrounded by Bangladesh on three sides?
questions.explanation: Tripura is surrounded by Bangladesh on its north, south, and west.

questions.text: The ocean which is named after a country is the:
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean is named after India because of India's long coastline and strategic position in the ocean.

questions.text: Which of the following is the largest state of India in terms of area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest state in India by geographical area.

questions.text: What is the degree of the Standard Meridian of India?
questions.explanation: 82°30'E is the longitude chosen as the Standard Meridian.

questions.text: How many states and Union Territories does India have currently?
questions.explanation: As per the current political map (post-2020), India has 28 states and 8 Union Territories.

questions.text: Which neighbor of India is located to the East?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh and Myanmar are India's neighbors to the East.

questions.text: The latitudinal extent of India influences:
options: The duration of day and night
questions.explanation: The latitudinal extent influences the duration of day and night, as one moves from south to north.

questions.text: Which geographical feature bounds India in the north-west, north and north-east?
questions.explanation: India is bounded by the young fold mountains in the north-west, north, and north-east.
